name: St. Paschal Baylons
description_short: St. Paschal Baylons Federal Credit Union is a member-focused financial institution established in 1957, serving religious group members in Highland Heights, Ohio with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking services.
description_long: St. Paschal Baylons Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and is headquartered in Highland Heights, Ohio. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit institution governed by and for its members, with a focus on serving members of religious groups and faith-based communities. The credit union offers a range of personal financial products including savings accounts, auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, mortgages, and share certificates (CDs). Members can access online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, bill pay services, and financial counseling. The institution maintains competitive interest rates, with current offerings including 3.69% for new vehicle loans and 5.21% for used vehicle loans, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional for-profit banks. St. Paschal Baylons distinguishes itself through its community-focused membership model and specialization in serving religious and faith-based groups. As a not-for-profit, it prioritizes member benefit over shareholder returns. The credit union stewards over $6 million in assets and serves more than 300 loyal members with a dedicated staff of over 2 employees across its single branch location. The organization also provides financial calculators, emergency fund guidance, and educational resources to support member financial literacy. The institution is relatively small with limited physical infrastructure—one branch, zero ATMs, and no share draft or money market accounts currently offered. While it provides essential banking services and competitive rates, prospective members should be aware that membership eligibility is restricted to religious group members, and the limited branch network may require members to rely on online and mobile banking for most transactions.
services: Service status monitoring and outage reporting
Community Q&A forum for member guidance
pros: Not-for-profit structure with competitive rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Competitive auto loan rates: 3.69% for new vehicles and 5.21% for used vehicles
Offers both online banking and mobile apps for iOS and Android devices
Provides financial counseling and educational resources including financial calculators
Member-focused community with personalized service for religious group members
Established institution with 67+ years of operation (since 1957)
Online bill pay and emergency fund guidance available to members
cons: Membership restricted to religious group members only—not open to general public
Only 1 branch location with 0 ATMs, limiting in-person access and cash availability
Very small institution with only 300+ members and 2+ employees, may lack resources
No share draft accounts or money market accounts currently available
Lobby currently closed at time of reporting, limiting walk-in service options
best_for: Members of religious organizations seeking member-owned financial institutions
Faith-based community members looking for competitive auto loan rates
Individuals comfortable with online and mobile banking without branch access
Credit union members prioritizing not-for-profit values and community focus
diagnosis: St. Paschal Baylons is best suited for members of religious groups seeking competitive rates and member-owned financial services with a community focus. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to religious organization members, and the single-branch, no-ATM structure requires comfort with digital banking and online transactions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
